Item 2. Management's Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations

Data Center revenue surpassed prior records as Blackwell architecture products entered volume production, with customer demand substantially exceeding our near-term supply capacity across major cloud and enterprise accounts.

Gaming revenue grew 16 percent year-over-year as the product refresh cycle continued, with desktop and laptop attach rates for our latest discrete GPU generation improving relative to prior launches.

Gross margin contracted modestly from peak levels as Blackwell production ramp introduced higher initial manufacturing costs, and we expect margin recovery as yields improve and throughput increases.

Export control restrictions imposed by the United States government on advanced semiconductor shipments to certain countries required us to develop alternative product configurations, reducing revenue opportunity in affected markets.

Operating expenses grew at a slower rate than revenue as scale benefits offset continued research and development investment and compensation increases driven by competition for specialized engineering talent.

Item 3. Other Information
